title: The Visible Spiral Plain

features.name: spiral-horizon
features.desc-on-touch: The escarpment's geometry continues into visible distance, spiralling outward as the land itself. You see the pattern from above become the shape you walk on.

features.name: far-ridge-memory
features.desc-on-touch: A distant ridge catches low light and holds it. You remember other high places the spiral connected to, places no path has reported from.

features.name: wind-grass-song
features.desc-on-touch: Wind across the high plain sounds like something learning to speak after long silence. The grass understands the pressure of altitude.

features.name: survey-cairn
features.desc-on-touch: Stacked stones mark a point where someone measured the land's curvature. The measurements never left this height.

features.name: mirage-archive
features.desc-on-touch: At certain angles the air holds the shapes of places below. The archive you descended from appears briefly on the horizon, then fades as you move.

features.name: plateau-edge-witness
features.desc-on-touch: The edge drops away cleanly. You feel the spiral's logic: pressure outward becomes the shape of falling, becomes distance. The land is always becoming the direction it was forced to go.

inscription: the spiral continues outward; you become the landscape it was always describing

file.name: SurveyNotes_HighPlateau_Theodolite_Records.txt
file.note: Measurements of the escarpment's spiral geometry, annotated with observations about how sight changes at altitude. The final entry fades mid-word.

exits.label: north along the ridge where the land gets stranger
east to where the spiral begins to flatten
